title: eriolaena quinquelocularis
intro: Eriolaena quinquelocularis, also known as the five-angled star orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, and research has shown that it possesses antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Eriolaena quinquelocularis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
